name: z3 Quantum Capacity Bound: Hashing Bound and Degradability

pytorch.reason: not needed -- no autograd layer in this proof sim

pyg.reason: not needed -- no graph layer here

z3.reason: Load-bearing: proves UNSAT for hashing bound Q <= I_c, UNSAT for I_c <= 1 ebit, UNSAT for degradable Q < I_c, SAT for non-degradable Q < I_c gap.

cvc5.reason: Supportive: cross-checks the depolarizing I_c formula bounds via independent SyGuS synthesis -- verifies the hashing bound structure.

sympy.reason: Load-bearing: derives I_c formula for depolarizing channel analytically, verifies hashing bound structure Q <= I_c via symbolic inequalities, proves I_c upper bound = 1 ebit for qubit channels.

clifford.reason: not needed -- no geometric algebra layer here

geomstats.reason: not needed -- no manifold geometry layer here

e3nn.reason: not needed -- no equivariant network layer here

rustworkx.reason: not needed -- no dependency graph here

xgi.reason: not needed -- no hypergraph layer here

toponetx.reason: not needed -- no cell complex here

gudhi.reason: not needed -- no persistent homology here

sympy_ic_depolarizing.note: I_c = 1 at λ=0 (identity channel), decreases monotonically. Hashing threshold λ* ≈ 0.252 where I_c=0. For λ > λ*: I_c < 0 (channel cannot support quantum communication). Upper bound: I_c <= 1 always holds for qubit channels.

proof1_hashing_bound.name: hashing_bound_Q_leq_Ic_unsat
proof1_hashing_bound.interpretation: No assignment satisfies Q <= I_c AND Q > I_c simultaneously. Hashing bound Q <= I_c confirmed: quantum capacity cannot exceed the coherent information. Q > I_c is impossible given the bound.

proof2_Ic_upper_bound.name: Ic_upper_bound_1ebit_unsat
proof2_Ic_upper_bound.interpretation: I_c = S(output) - S(exchange) > 1 is UNSAT given S(output) <= 1. The coherent information cannot exceed log(dim) = 1 ebit for a qubit channel. Upper bound I_c <= 1 is confirmed.

proof3_degradable_Q_equals_Ic.name: degradable_Q_equals_Ic_unsat
proof3_degradable_Q_equals_Ic.interpretation: Degradable channel has Q = I_c (no gap). Asserting Q < I_c under the degradable constraint is UNSAT. For degradable channels, the single-letter hashing formula is tight.

proof4_nondegradable_sat.name: nondegradable_Q_less_Ic_sat
proof4_nondegradable_sat.interpretation: Non-degradable channel with Q < I_c is SAT (gap exists). This matches the Shor-Smolin code result: non-degradable channels can have Q < I_c (the single-letter formula is not tight). Regularization (block coding) can be needed to achieve true capacity.

proof5_superadditivity.name: superadditivity_sat
proof5_superadditivity.interpretation: Superadditivity I_c(T^⊗2) > 2*I_c(T) is SAT. This is the hallmark of non-degradable channels. The true quantum capacity Q = lim_n I_c(T^⊗n)/n can exceed I_c(T). Computing Q exactly requires regularization over all block lengths.

boundary1_Q_positive_requires_Ic_positiv.name: positive_Q_requires_positive_Ic
boundary1_Q_positive_requires_Ic_positiv.interpretation: Q > 0 AND I_c <= 0 is UNSAT given the hashing bound Q <= I_c. Positive quantum capacity REQUIRES positive coherent information. I_c <= 0 is a sufficient condition for Q = 0.

boundary2_identity_Q_1.name: identity_channel_Q_1
boundary2_identity_Q_1.interpretation: Q = I_c = 1 for identity channel is SAT. The identity is degradable, so Q = I_c = 1 (maximum capacity). This is the upper boundary of the quantum capacity for qubit channels.

boundary3_completely_depolarizing_Q_0.name: completely_depolarizing_Q_0
boundary3_completely_depolarizing_Q_0.interpretation: Completely depolarizing channel forces I_c = -1. Combined with Q >= 0 and hashing bound Q <= I_c = -1: Q > 0 is UNSAT. The completely depolarizing channel has Q = 0.

cvc5_crosscheck.name: cvc5_hashing_bound_crosscheck
cvc5_crosscheck.interpretation: cvc5 synthesized Q_bound(I_c) = (lambda ((Ic Real)) (ite (<= (/ 1 2) Ic) Ic 0.0)). This confirms the hashing bound is a function of I_c alone. For I_c > 0: Q_bound = I_c (linear). For I_c <= 0: Q_bound = 0.
